reasoning: The weather station code 71805 refers to Saint-Pierre in Saint-Pierre and Miquelon. This WMO station block (71) is unambiguously located in North America. Since the forecast traces dates from late October into late January, the location is entering Northern Hemisphere winter, meaning average daily temperatures will steadily and significantly decrease. While weather variability (standard deviation roughly 4 to 6°C) leaves a fair chance for November 2 to be warmer than October 26 (~41%), this chance drops substantially by November 25 as the mean expected temperature decreases by ~5°C (~22%). By January 24, average temperatures fall well below freezing, making it highly improbable to exceed the ambient temperature of late October (~9%). Although general forecasting heuristics suggest pulling distant probabilities closer to 0.5 for random walk variables, deterministic seasonal cycles dictate that probabilities approach 0 here. I have prioritized physical realism for maximum expected accuracy.
